sections_cited: section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI ITRA 10 of 2024 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on office objection No.27 2. For hearing of main case 18.11.2025 Mr. Munawar Ali Memon, advocate for applicant Mr. Shams Mohiuddin Ansari, advocate for respondent Per learned counsel, the impugned judgment is not a speaking order as it contains no independent deliberation, discussion and/or reasoning. Learned counsel states that dealing with the lis, the tribunal has rendered the judgment in a perfunctory manner and the same is not befitting the last fact-finding forum in the statutory hierarchy. Learned counsel for respondent states that in view of the forgoing he has no cavil if the impugned judgment is set aside and the matter is remanded back to the Tribunal for adjudication afresh in accordance with law expeditiously, preferably within three months. He further seeks that pending the foregoing no coercive action be taken against the respondent arising here from. Learned counsel for the applicant states that this reference application may be disposed of in the said terms. Order accordingly. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001. Judge Judge Amjad
